key_drivers: DXY traded at 98.074 and was down 0.30% on the day, preserving a weaker-USD backdrop across metals, equities, and G10 FX [1].
VIX printed 18.13 and fell 5.18%, leaving the regime balanced by level but supportive for equities through falling volatility [2].
Atlanta Fed GDPNow stood at 1.3% for 2026:Q1, implying a softer U.S. growth backdrop that supports defensive metals but complicates cyclicals [3].
The EIA reported a 3.1 million barrel crude build, but Hormuz-related disruptions remain a material bullish supply shock for oil [4] [5].
China's March manufacturing PMI returned to expansion at 50.4, while copper futures were higher, reinforcing the AUD and pro-cyclical commodity complex [11] [12].
